playTicTacToe.name: Build the Tic Tac Toe game
playTicTacToe.description: Create a runGame(moves) function that draws a board, places pieces, handles invalid moves, detects wins and draws, and implements basic AI for '?' moves.

partialGame.name: The first few moves of a game
partialGame.description: Draw the grid and place the first few pieces correctly. The game is still in progress.

draw.name: A drawn game
draw.description: Play a full game that ends in a draw. All pieces should be greyed out with a draw message.

doublePlacementDuplicate.name: Placing an 'o' on an 'o'
doublePlacementDuplicate.description: Placing a piece on an already occupied square should show an error.

doublePlacementOnTop.name: Placing an 'x' on an 'o'
doublePlacementOnTop.description: Placing a piece on top of an opponent's piece should show an error.

winX.name: The crosses win!
winX.description: Play a game where the crosses win. The winning line should be highlighted.

winO.name: The noughts win!
winO.description: Play a game where the noughts win. The winning circles should be highlighted.

aiOFinish.name: AI 'o' finish
aiOFinish.description: Can your AI finish the game successfully?

aiXWin.name: AI 'x' win
aiXWin.description: Can your AI win a game for crosses?

aiOWin.name: AI 'o' win
aiOWin.description: Can your AI win a game for noughts?

aiXBlock.name: AI 'x' block
aiXBlock.description: Can your AI block the opponent's win?

aiGame.name: AI game
aiGame.description: Can your AI play this game out to a draw?

whereToStart.question: Where should I start with such a big exercise?
whereToStart.answer: Break it up. First draw the empty grid (one rectangle plus four lines). Then write helper functions for drawing an `o` and an `x`. Build the game logic on top of those.

trackSquares.question: How do I keep track of which squares are taken?
trackSquares.answer: Use a 2D list (a list of three lists, each with three entries) where each cell holds `"o"`, `"x"`, or an empty marker. Update it every time a move is made.

whoseTurn.question: How do I know whose turn it is?
whoseTurn.answer: The first move is always `"o"`, then it alternates. Track the move number, or flip a variable between `"o"` and `"x"` after each placement.

detectWin.question: How do I detect a win?
detectWin.answer: After each move, check all 8 winning lines: 3 rows, 3 columns, and 2 diagonals. For each, check whether all three cells contain the same non-empty symbol.

aiMove.question: How does the AI decide what to do for a `"?"` move?
aiMove.answer: Try the rules in order: first see if there's a move that wins immediately, then see if there's one that blocks the opponent's win, otherwise pick any empty square.

rectangle.description: Draw a rectangle at position (left, top) with the given width, height, and color

circle.description: Draw a circle centered at (centerX, centerY) with the given radius and color

line.description: Draw a line from (x1, y1) to (x2, y2) with the given color

changeStroke.description: Set the stroke width and color for subsequent shapes. Lines are drawn using the stroke color.

write.description: Write text to the center of the screen in white
